zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Ann Bailey於1835年11月20日出生在美國阿拉巴馬州時，她的父親John Bailey21歲，母親Elisabeth Land20歲。她於1853年7月15日與Joshua Allen Griffith在美國阿拉巴馬州馬歇爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她於1850年住在美國阿拉巴馬州德卡爾布。她去世於1889年3月8日在美國阿肯色州蘭多夫享年53歲，埋葬在美國阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Ann Bailey于1835年11月20日出生在美国亚拉巴马州时，她的父亲John Bailey21岁，母亲Elisabeth Land20岁。她于1853年7月15日与Joshua Allen Griffith在美国亚拉巴马州馬歇爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她于1850年住在美国亚拉巴马州德卡爾布。她去世于1889年3月8日在美国阿肯色州蘭多夫享年53岁，埋葬在美国阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Bailey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

variants.name: Bailly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Marcel, Philippe.
French: occupational name for a steward or official, from Old French baillif. Compare Bailey 4.
French: habitational name from (Le) Bailly, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Bale
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, Middle English bailli, baile, from Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’ (see Bailey 3).
Breton (Balé): variant of Balay .
French: unexplained.

variants.name: Baily
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayly
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Balay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Rajesh, Satish.
Altered form of English   Bailey  .
French: habitational name from any of various minor places so named.

variants.name: Baile
meanings: Spanish: status name for a steward or official, from Old Spanish baile, Late Latin baiulivus; cognate with English Bailey .
French (southern): variant of   Bail  .
English: variant of   Bale  .

variants.name: Baillie
meanings: Scottish:

from Older Scots baillie, baili. In Scotland, a baillie was a status name for any of various officials, including the chief magistrate in a barony and (later) the chief magistrate or administrative officer of a barony or district. Etymologically, this is the same word as English Bailey , but the functions as well as the spelling of the two officials were different in England and Scotland.
topographic name from Older Scots bailli ‘outer court of a castle’; see Bailey 3 and Bale 1.

variants.name: Baley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.
Historical Boundaries: 1836: DeKalb, Alabama, United States
Arkansas supplied an estimated 50,000 men to the Confederate Army andabout 15,000 to the Union Army.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: Skirmish at Benton
historicEvents.description: Confederate forces in Arkansas began an invasion of Missouri, while other Confederate sources probed the line around Little Rock. On July  6, 1864 the fourth Arkansas Cavalry tried to break the line around Little Rock one soldier was killed, eight were wounded, three went missing from the Union side and four were killed and six wounded from the Confederate side.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture-Skirmish at Benton

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln is assassinated by John Wilkes Booth.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

historicEvents.title: The Mosaic Templar is Founded
historicEvents.description: The Mosaic Templar is an African American fraternal organization founded in Little Rock. it was founded by former slaves, John Edward Bush and Chester W. Keatts. It was part of a movement that was going on at the time, where everyone was forming fraternities and sororities. The main departments for this one where endowment, monument, analysis, uniform, rank, recapitulation, records, and a juvenile division.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture- Mosaic Templars of America

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Ann Bailey was born on 20 November 1835, in Alabama, United States, her father, John Bailey, was 21 and her mother, Elisabeth Land, was 20. She married Joshua Allen Griffith on 15 July 1853, in Marshall, Alabama, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She lived in DeKalb, Alabama, United States in 1850. She died on 8 March 1889, in Randolph, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 53, and was buried in Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Baileyが1835年11月20日にアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州で生まれた時，父John Baileyは21歳，母Elisabeth Landは20歳であった。1853年7月15日にアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州マーシャルでJoshua Allen Griffithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州デカルブに住んでいた。彼女は1889年3月8日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ランドルフで53歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラウレンスラベンデン スプリングズジャンス セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Bailey(이)가 1835년 11월 20일 미국 앨라배마 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bailey(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth Land(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1853년 7월 15일 미국 앨라배마 주 마샤에서 Joshua Allen Griffith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 앨라배마 주 데캘브에서 살았다. 그녀는 1889년 3월 8일 미국 아칸소 주 랜돌프에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스 제네스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Ann Bailey nasceu em 20 de novembro de 1835, em Alabama, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, John Bailey, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth Land, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Joshua Allen Griffith em 15 de julho de 1853, em Marshall, Alabama, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela viveu em DeKalb, Alabama, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1889, em Randolph, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 53 anos, e foi sepultada em Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Ann Bailey родилась 20 ноября 1835 г. в местности Алабама, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени John Bailey было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Land, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Joshua Allen Griffith 15 июля 1853 г. в местности маршолл, Алабама, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она жила в местности декалб, Алабама, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла 8 марта 1889 г. в местности рандолф, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности дженесь кеметири, равенден спрингс, ларенс, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Ann Bailey nació el 20 de noviembre de 1835, en Alabama, Estados Unidos, su papá, John Bailey, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Elisabeth Land, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Joshua Allen Griffith el 15 de julio de 1853, en Marshall, Alabama, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Vivió en DeKalb, Alabama, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1889, en Randolph, Arkansas, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Estados Unidos.
